entries.text: OK, OK, let's go with the last module.

Okay, so the last module is about automatic scaling and monitoring. We're going to see three main things. Load balancing, and again, the main AWS service for load balancing, which is Elastic Load Balancer, ELB, then CloudWatch,
which is a monitoring tool, and then auto scaling, which is the Amazon AWS service for scaling in and out EC2 instances.
Okay, so let's start with load balancing.

So Elastic Load Balancing, this is the AWS service for load balancing. As any load balancer, it distributes incoming application or network traffic. So we will see that it is application load balancer and network load balancer, which differs in the type of traffic that they distribute, but it necessarily a load balancer distributes.

Incoming traffic across multiple targets. These targets typically are EC2 instances in a single availability zone or across multiple availability zones. Okay, scales your load balancer as traffic to your application changes over time, so...
The elastic load balancer itself scales also automatically as traffic changes over time.

So there is these three types actually, the now, so at this time in 2022, AWS mainly uses two type of load balancer, which is the application load balancer and the network load balancer. And previously, they had just a generic load balancer that could be used for both.
application traffic and network layer traffic. Okay. But now they have this distinction. You can set up.

Application load balancer, which will load balance HTTP and HTTPS traffic, so layer 7 application layer traffic, or the network load balancer that will load balance transport layer traffic, so TCP, UDP, and TLS traffic. Okay.

The application load balancer routes traffic to targets based on content of the request. And then the network load balancer routes traffic to targets based on IP protocol data. Okay. These are the application load balancer provides advanced request routing targeted at the delivery of modern obligation architectures, including microservices and containers.
Okay, and the network load balancer.

can handle this is the throughput of this one is much higher, millions of requests per second while maintaining ultra low latencies and is optimised to handle sudden and volatile traffic patterns. of the targets and load balancer routes traffic only to the healthy targets.

So what you want is that the provision capacity, your supply matches your demand as close as possible. So this is a concept of elasticity. Helps you maintain application availability, enables you to automatically add or remove instances according to conditions that you define.
